Indexer operations notes
========================

These notes are plain text on purpose: they exercise the chunker's
line-based fallback path, where no tree-sitter grammar applies.

First indexing run
------------------
The first run over a repository is always a full pass: every discovered
file is hashed, chunked, embedded, and upserted. Expect this to be
dominated by embedding time, not parsing time. On a laptop CPU the
embedder is the bottleneck by an order of magnitude.

Incremental runs
----------------
Subsequent runs re-hash every candidate file but only re-chunk and
re-embed the files whose content hash changed. Deleted files drop all
of their chunks. Renames currently look like a delete plus an add;
that is acceptable for now and cheap to revisit later.

Operational tips
----------------
1. Keep the Qdrant container on a local SSD; vector upserts are the
   second-largest cost after embedding.
2. Watch the failed-files count in the dashboard after big refactors.
   A spike usually means a grammar regression, not corrupt files.
3. When the embedding model changes, the whole index is invalid.
   Force a full re-index rather than mixing vector spaces.
